car.notes: Edinburgh Airport sits on the city's western edge — convenient for picking up on the way out and dropping back before the Sunday flight.

days.title: Edinburgh → Glencoe → Fort William
notes: Pick up the car at Enterprise (EDI) at 9:30 am, then head straight west.
October daylight fades around 6:30 pm — front-load the scenic driving.
Scenic M9/A82 via Stirling, Loch Lomond and Rannoch Moor.
At the Wee White House use the roadside bays — the main car park has a notorious pothole, and the ground near the river is boggy.
Stop at the Three Sisters viewpoint while you're in the glen.
Quick run up the A82 along Loch Linnhe.
Torlinnhe check-in from 3:00 pm — loch-view room.

days.title: Glenfinnan → Eilean Donan → Skye
notes: Torlinnhe checkout is 10:00 am — leave promptly to make the steam train.
Single-track sections on the A87; build in a little buffer.
Be at the viewpoint car park by ~10:15 for the ~10:45 Jacobite steam-train crossing.
The car park fills fast and closes when full; small National Trust parking fee.
Confirm the 2026 train schedule on the West Coast Railways site before counting on it.
Back through Fort William, up the A82 then the A87 through dramatic Glen Shiel.
Castle open 9–5; allow about an hour inside; best photos near low tide.
Plockton is a lovely ~20-minute detour if you have time.
Cross the Skye Bridge at Kyle of Lochalsh.
The Bosville check-in from 3:00 pm.

days.title: Skye → Edinburgh
notes: Leave Skye by early afternoon so the single-track stretches aren't in the dark.
Fit in Old Man of Storr or Kilt Rock in the morning before heading off.
Retrace the A87 past Eilean Donan, then south on the A82.
Stop in Fort William or Glencoe for a late lunch and a leg-stretch.
Re-pass Glencoe and Rannoch Moor — completely different in the afternoon light.
Premier Inn is by the airport; return the car to Enterprise at 9:00 am Sunday.
